Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005000168 == Commons category (P373) == * Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Sakino Shrine|lt=Sakino Shrine|WD=Q135038846}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former designated {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = {{jalink|Hakuhō|Q11580612}} 3 ({{jalink|674|Q30302}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{jalink|Saki-chō}}, {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|small Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|village shrine|Q712534|village shrine}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{jalink|heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{jalink|Hakuhō|Q11580612}} 3 ({{jalink|674|Q30302}}), it was ranked an {{jalink|official shrine|Q11452104|official shrine}} in {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 3 ({{jalink|891|Q30580}}), and later {{jalink|Chōshō-ji}} became its {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{jalink|Jishō|Q1194671}} 4 ({{jalink|1180|Q19702}}) due to {{jalink|Taira no Shigehira|Q710537}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} 6 ({{jalink|1190|Q19736}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 6 ({{jalink|1578|Q6707}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{jalink|Benzaiten|Q818468|Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{jalink|Meireki|Q1192766}} 2 ({{jalink|1656|Q6982}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{jalink|Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{jalink|Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|Haraedo-sha}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{jalink|Nara National Museum|Q147312}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{jalink|Marishiten|Q3273446}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{jalink|Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{jalink|Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293}} {{jalink|Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{jalink|Twelve Devas|Q9503981}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|Akadōji}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{jalink|gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|gold mud}} {{jalink|Lotus Sutra|Q861212}} Fumon chapter === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === {{osm box|w|341688901}} {{commonscat|Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata)|Saki Shrine}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{jalink|Meicho Publishing}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City, Temples and Shrines|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * Origin board displayed in precinct {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005000168 == Commons category (P373) == * Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Sakino Shrine|lt=Sakino Shrine|WD=Q135038846}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former designated {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = {{jalink|Hakuhō|Q11580612}} 3 ({{jalink|674|Q30302}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{jalink|Saki-chō}}, {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|small Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|village shrine|Q712534|village shrine}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{jalink|heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{jalink|Hakuhō|Q11580612}} 3 ({{jalink|674|Q30302}}), it was ranked an {{jalink|official shrine|Q11452104|official shrine}} in {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 3 ({{jalink|891|Q30580}}), and later {{jalink|Chōshō-ji}} became its {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{jalink|Jishō|Q1194671}} 4 ({{jalink|1180|Q19702}}) due to {{jalink|Taira no Shigehira|Q710537}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} 6 ({{jalink|1190|Q19736}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 6 ({{jalink|1578|Q6707}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{jalink|Benzaiten|Q818468|Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{jalink|Meireki|Q1192766}} 2 ({{jalink|1656|Q6982}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{jalink|Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{jalink|Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|Haraedo-sha}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{jalink|Nara National Museum|Q147312}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{jalink|Marishiten|Q3273446}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{jalink|Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{jalink|Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293}} {{jalink|Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{jalink|Twelve Devas|Q9503981}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|Akadōji}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{jalink|gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|gold mud}} {{jalink|Lotus Sutra|Q861212}} Fumon chapter === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === {{osm box|w|341688901}} {{commonscat|Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata)|Saki Shrine}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{jalink|Meicho Publishing}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City, Temples and Shrines|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * Origin board displayed in precinct {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005000168 == Commons category (P373) == * Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Sakino Shrine|lt=Sakino Shrine|WD=Q135038846}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{ill||lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}・{{ill||lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) / former designated {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{ill||lt=Saki-chō|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{ill|lt=heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}), it was ranked an {{ill|lt=official shrine|Q11452104|WD=Q11452104|lt=official shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 3 ({{ill|lt=891|Q30580|WD=Q30580}}), and later {{ill||lt=Chōshō-ji|UNKNOWN}} became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{ill|lt=Jishō|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}}) due to {{ill|lt=Taira no Shigehira|Q710537|WD=Q710537}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|WD=Q6982}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Haraedo-sha}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|lt=Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Marishiten|Q3273446|WD=Q3273446}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877|WD=Q2661877}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293|WD=Q2671293}} {{ill|lt=Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329|WD=Q842329}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Twelve Devas|Q9503981|WD=Q9503981}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|lt=Akadōji}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{ill|lt=gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|WD=Q11647560|lt=gold mud}} {{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}} Fumon chapter === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === {{osm box|w|341688901}} {{commonscat|Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata)|Saki Shrine}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill||lt=Meicho Publishing|UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City, Temples and Shrines|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * Origin board displayed in precinct {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005000168 == Commons category (P373) == * Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Sakino Shrine|lt=Sakino Shrine|WD=Q135038846}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) / former designated {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saki-chō|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{ill|lt=heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}), it was ranked an {{ill|lt=official shrine|Q11452104|WD=Q11452104|lt=official shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 3 ({{ill|lt=891|Q30580|WD=Q30580}}), and later {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōshō-ji|UNKNOWN}} became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{ill|lt=Jishō|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}}) due to {{ill|lt=Taira no Shigehira|Q710537|WD=Q710537}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|WD=Q6982}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Haraedo-sha}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|lt=Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Marishiten|Q3273446|WD=Q3273446}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877|WD=Q2661877}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293|WD=Q2671293}} {{ill|lt=Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329|WD=Q842329}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Twelve Devas|Q9503981|WD=Q9503981}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|lt=Akadōji}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{ill|lt=gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|WD=Q11647560|lt=gold mud}} {{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}} Fumon chapter === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === {{osm box|w|341688901}} Publishing|UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City, Temples and Shrines|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * Origin board displayed in precinct {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005000168 == Commons category (P373) == * Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Sakino Shrine|lt=Sakino Shrine|WD=Q135038846}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) / former designated {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saki-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{ill|lt=heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}), it was ranked an {{ill|lt=official shrine|Q11452104|WD=Q11452104|lt=official shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 3 ({{ill|lt=891|Q30580|WD=Q30580}}), and later {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōshō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{ill|lt=Jishō|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}}) due to {{ill|lt=Taira no Shigehira|Q710537|WD=Q710537}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|WD=Q6982}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Haraedo-sha}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|lt=Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Marishiten|Q3273446|WD=Q3273446}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877|WD=Q2661877}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293|WD=Q2671293}} {{ill|lt=Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329|WD=Q842329}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Twelve Devas|Q9503981|WD=Q9503981}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|lt=Akadōji}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{ill|lt=gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|WD=Q11647560|lt=gold mud}} {{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}} Fumon chapter === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === {{osm box|w|341688901}} {{commonscat|Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata)|Saki Shrine}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Publishing|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City, Temples and Shrines|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * Origin board displayed in precinct {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] Shrine|WD=Q135038846}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former designated {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saki-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{ill|lt=heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}), it was ranked an {{ill|lt=official shrine|Q11452104|WD=Q11452104|lt=official shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 3 ({{ill|lt=891|Q30580|WD=Q30580}}), and later {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōshō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{ill|lt=Jishō|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}}) due to {{ill|lt=Taira no Shigehira|Q710537|WD=Q710537}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|WD=Q6982}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Haraedo-sha}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|lt=Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Marishiten|Q3273446|WD=Q3273446}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877|WD=Q2661877}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293|WD=Q2671293}} {{ill|lt=Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329|WD=Q842329}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Twelve Devas|Q9503981|WD=Q9503981}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|lt=Akadōji}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{ill|lt=gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|WD=Q11647560|lt=gold mud}} {{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}} Fumon chapter === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === {{osm box|w|341688901}} {{commonscat|Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata)|Saki Shrine}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Publishing|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City, Temples and Shrines|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * Origin board displayed in precinct {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135038846}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former designated {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saki-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{ill|lt=heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}), it was ranked an {{ill|lt=official shrine|Q11452104|WD=Q11452104|lt=official shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 3 ({{ill|lt=891|Q30580|WD=Q30580}}), and later {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōshō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{ill|lt=Jishō|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}}) due to {{ill|lt=Taira no Shigehira|Q710537|WD=Q710537}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|WD=Q6982}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Haraedo-sha}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|lt=Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Marishiten|Q3273446|WD=Q3273446}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877|WD=Q2661877}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293|WD=Q2671293}} {{ill|lt=Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329|WD=Q842329}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Twelve Devas|Q9503981|WD=Q9503981}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|lt=Akadōji}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{ill|lt=gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|WD=Q11647560|lt=gold mud}} {{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}} Fumon chapter === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === {{osm box|w|341688901}} Publishing|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City, Temples and Shrines|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * Origin board displayed in precinct {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005000168 == Commons category (P373) == * Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Sakino Shrine|lt=Sakino Shrine|WD=Q135038846}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former designated {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saki-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{ill|lt=heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}), it was ranked an {{ill|lt=official shrine|Q11452104|WD=Q11452104|lt=official shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 3 ({{ill|lt=891|Q30580|WD=Q30580}}), and later {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōshō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{ill|lt=Jishō|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}}) due to {{ill|lt=Taira no Shigehira|Q710537|WD=Q710537}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|WD=Q6982}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Haraedo-sha}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|lt=Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Marishiten|Q3273446|WD=Q3273446}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877|WD=Q2661877}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293|WD=Q2671293}} {{ill|lt=Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329|WD=Q842329}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Twelve Devas|Q9503981|WD=Q9503981}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|lt=Akadōji}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{ill|lt=gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|WD=Q11647560|lt=gold mud}} {{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}} Fumon chapter === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === {{osm box|w|341688901}} {{commonscat|Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata)|Saki Shrine}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Publishing|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City, Temples and Shrines|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * Origin board displayed in precinct {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135038846}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former designated {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saki-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{ill|lt=heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}), it was ranked an {{ill|lt=official shrine|Q11452104|WD=Q11452104|lt=official shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 3 ({{ill|lt=891|Q30580|WD=Q30580}}), and later {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōshō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{ill|lt=Jishō|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}}) due to {{ill|lt=Taira no Shigehira|Q710537|WD=Q710537}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|WD=Q6982}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Haraedo-sha}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|lt=Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Marishiten|Q3273446|WD=Q3273446}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877|WD=Q2661877}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293|WD=Q2671293}} {{ill|lt=Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329|WD=Q842329}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Twelve Devas|Q9503981|WD=Q9503981}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|lt=Akadōji}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{ill|lt=gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|WD=Q11647560|lt=gold mud}} {{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}} Fumon chapter === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === {{osm box|w|341688901}} {{commonscat|Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata)|Saki Shrine}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Publishing|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City, Temples and Shrines|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * Origin board displayed in precinct {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135038846}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former designated {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saki-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{ill|lt=heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}), it was ranked an {{ill|lt=official shrine|Q11452104|WD=Q11452104|lt=official shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 3 ({{ill|lt=891|Q30580|WD=Q30580}}), and later {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōshō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{ill|lt=Jishō|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}}) due to {{ill|lt=Taira no Shigehira|Q710537|WD=Q710537}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{ill|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|WD=Q6982}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Haraedo-sha}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|lt=Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Marishiten|Q3273446|WD=Q3273446}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877|WD=Q2661877}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293|WD=Q2671293}} {{ill|lt=Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329|WD=Q842329}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Twelve Devas|Q9503981|WD=Q9503981}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|lt=Akadōji}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{ill|lt=gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|WD=Q11647560|lt=gold mud}} Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005000168 == Commons category (P373) == * Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Sakino Shrine|lt=Sakino Shrine|WD=Q135038846}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former designated {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saki-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{ill|lt=heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302}}), it was ranked an {{ill|lt=official shrine|Q11452104|WD=Q11452104|lt=official shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 3 ({{ill|lt=891|Q30580|WD=Q30580}}), and later {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōshō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{ill|lt=Jishō|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}}) due to {{ill|lt=Taira no Shigehira|Q710537|WD=Q710537}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{ill|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|WD=Q6982}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Haraedo-sha}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|lt=Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Marishiten|Q3273446|WD=Q3273446}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877|WD=Q2661877}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005000168 == Commons category (P373) == * Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Sakino Shrine|lt=Sakino Shrine|WD=Q135038846|1=Sakino Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県奈良市佐紀町1716', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former designated {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saki-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{ill|lt=heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}), it was ranked an {{ill|lt=official shrine|Q11452104|WD=Q11452104|lt=official shrine|1=kokuheisha}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=891|Q30580|WD=Q30580|1=891}}), and later {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōshō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{ill|lt=Jishō|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671|1=Jishō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702|1=1180}}) due to {{ill|lt=Taira no Shigehira|Q710537|WD=Q710537|1=Taira no Shigehira}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736|1=1190}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{ill|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|WD=Q6982|1=1656}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Haraedo-sha|1=Haraedo no Kami}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312|1=Nara National Museum}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|lt=Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Marishiten|Q3273446|WD=Q3273446|1=Marici (Buddhism)}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877|WD=Q2661877|1=Sūryaprabha}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293|WD=Q2671293|1=Candraprabha}} {{ill|lt=Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329|WD=Q842329|1=Twelve Heavenly Generals}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Twelve Devas|Q9503981|WD=Q9503981|1=Jūniten}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|lt=Akadōji|1=Kasuga-taisha}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{ill|lt=gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|WD=Q11647560|lt=gold mud|1=Gold paint-Gindei}} {{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}} Fumon chapter === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Publishing|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City, Temples and Shrines|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * Origin board displayed in precinct {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきしんしや}} == Categories == Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Saki Shrine (Kamehata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字亀畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346760}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}Saki-chō 1716 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former designated {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 44.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 32.91 |地図 = Nara city}} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamehata, aza Saki-chō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saki-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} under old shrine ranking, and a {{ill|lt=heihaku-offering shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine designated for sacred food/silk offerings|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === In works such as 'Jinmyōchō Kōshō,' 'Jingishi-ryō,' and 'Tokusen Jinmyōchō,' this shrine is identified with Saki Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} entries{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}Origin board in precinct. Founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}), it was ranked an {{ill|lt=official shrine|Q11452104|WD=Q11452104|lt=official shrine|1=kokuheisha}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=891|Q30580|WD=Q30580|1=891}}), and later {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōshō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It burned in {{ill|lt=Jishō|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671|1=Jishō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702|1=1180}}) due to {{ill|lt=Taira no Shigehira|Q710537|WD=Q710537|1=Taira no Shigehira}}'s burning, but was revived together with Chōshō-ji in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736|1=1190}}) by petition of Nakamikado Ietada{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305-306}}. It burned again in warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), and though rebuilt, shrine lands became smaller{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * Six Mikata Deities - regarded as deities seated in six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, with three associated seats{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. Beside it is a foundation stone carved with round seat, considered from Chōshō-ji{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Haiden === Two-ken four-sided, tiled roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata) Keidaisha1.jpg|thumb|200px|Benzaiten small shrine]] * {{ill|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}} small shrine - south-facing before torii, enshrines three Benzaiten deities{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. In front is stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|WD=Q6982|1=1656}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * Massha {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=Ichikishima-hime}} Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} Shrine - east of honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Haraedo-sha|1=Haraedo no Kami}} === Treasures === Treasures related to Chōshō-ji are preserved and all deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312|1=Nara National Museum}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=306}}. * Pair of yosegi-wood painted Komainu * One green staff said to have been secretly treasured by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|lt=Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Marishiten|Q3273446|WD=Q3273446|1=Marici (Buddhism)}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Nikkō Bosatsu|Q2661877|WD=Q2661877|1=Sūryaprabha}} two divine generals image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Gakkō Bosatsu|Q2671293|WD=Q2671293|1=Candraprabha}} {{ill|lt=Twelve Divine Generals|Q842329|WD=Q842329|1=Twelve Heavenly Generals}} image * One hanging scroll, silk, colored {{ill|lt=Twelve Devas|Q9503981|WD=Q9503981|1=Jūniten}} image * One hanging scroll, paper, colored {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|lt=Akadōji|1=Kasuga-taisha}} image * One scroll, dark-blue paper with {{ill|lt=gold and silver mud ink|Q11647560|WD=Q11647560|lt=gold mud|1=Gold paint-Gindei}} {{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}} Fumon chapter === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === {{osm box|w|341688901}} {{commonscat|Saki Shrine (Nara, Kamehata)|Saki Shrine}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Publishing|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City, Temples and Shrines|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * Origin board displayed in precinct {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきしんしや}} == Categories == Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
